title: Update working memory from session
summary: Durable context learned during the current coding session.

claims.text: getOverviewKPIs now computes the main KPI row, distinct subagents, distinct skills, distinct slash commands, and all-time total sessions with five concurrent ClickHouse queries via Promise.all instead of one query containing scalar ARRAY JOIN subqueries.

getOverviewInsights now launches the current-period stats, previous-period stats, top-performer, and knowledge-silo ClickHouse queries together in one Promise.all; the Postgres user-name lookup still runs afterward only when a top performer exists.

The overview latency fix intentionally targeted the two high-impact query-ordering problems first: parallelizing the KPI scans and the insights ClickHouse queries, without adding analytics result caching in this slice.

A short in-memory TTL cache remains an optional future optimization for repeat analytics dashboard loads if single-query ClickHouse scan cost is still too high after the overview query parallelization.
Error trends now accept only project_path, user_id, and model split values and default to project_path; repository and error_type are not valid error-trend split values.

ErrorsPage initializes the Errors over time chart with project_path splitting, and ErrorTrendChart labels that split as Project while displaying only the last path segment for project_path dimensions.

ChartLegend is the shared recharts legend renderer for scrollable right-side legends: it accepts a readonly payload, optional formatter, optional hiddenSeries set, and optional onToggle callback, and renders each entry as a button whose hidden state is shown with lower opacity and strikethrough text.

Success Rate surfaces use an aggregate tooltip describing the average session quality score formula, while individual session Score surfaces use a separate tooltip describing the per-session 0-100 score inputs and deductions.

ConversationMessage renders slash-command Command and Args code chips with bg-secondary text-foreground because the theme defines muted and muted-foreground as the same color, making bg-muted chips look redacted under muted foreground text.

The PR title workflow uses amannn/action-semantic-pull-request with validateSingleCommit and validateSingleCommitMatchesPrTitle enabled, so a one-commit PR must have a conventional PR title that matches the single commit subject.
Pull-request CI runs bunx turbo run lint check-types test build, so chart and dashboard changes must satisfy Biome lint and the apps/web TypeScript build before the verify job passes.

sources.title: Claude Code session: overview latency and chart dashboard UX

metadata.reason: The session analyzed three performance options, and the user explicitly asked to implement fixes 1 and 2: parallelize insights and KPI queries.
The session identified a two-to-five-minute in-memory cache as an optional next optimization for repeat dashboard loads, but the accepted implementation scope did not include caching.
